sections.paragraph:
or אֲפִיק always in constr. st. אֲפִיק, pl. אֲפִיקֵי m.-

(1) a channel, tube, so called from the idea of containing (see אָפַק No. 1 ). Job 40:18, אֲפִיקֵי נְחוּשָׁה “tubes of brass.”-(a) a channel, bed of a stream, Isa 8:7 Eze 32:6 also, the bottom of the sea, 2Sa 22:16.-(b) a brook, a stream, Psa 42:2 126:4 Joe 1:20. אֲפִיק נְחָלִים “stream of the vallies,” Job 6:15. Hence-(c) a valley itself, especially as watered by a stream, i.q. נַחַל, Arab. وادى [wâdy], Eze 6:3 34:13 35:8 36:4 , Eze 36:6.

(2) strong, robust, see the root No. 2. Job 41:7, אֲפִיקֵי מָגִנִּים “the strong of shields,” i.e. strong shields (of a crocodile). Job 12:21, “he looseth the girdle of the strong.” Par. נְדִיבִים. The notion of swiftness is attributed to this word by Ewald, on Son 5:12 but this is arbitrary.
